My current interests include sharing my statistical inquiries and technical reports I’ve worked that may help others solve similar problems related to applied statistics.

title: About
text:
🚧 This website is actively being built. Some pages may be incomplete or move.
Hello! I’m Vince Pileggi, a wastewater engineering expert, with the Ontario Ministry fo the Environment, Conservation and Parks and adjunct professor at the University of Guelph, School of Engineering.
Aside from my regular work I’m particularly passionate about the correct application of statistics to solving engineering problems when appropriate. I use RStudio [@rstudio] for R development, Quarto [@quarto] for technical publishing and of course \(\LaTeX\) [@latex] for high-quality typesetting.
My dissertation was focused on municipal sludge treatment using an anaerobic membrane bioreactor and is available though my Research Gate profile [@pileggi_researchgate] and at the Uni. The thesis was written in \(LaTeX\) but included as significant amount of R-coding and statistics. It was a lot of fun with some frustration finishing it off 😄!
This project hosts statistical analyses and technical notes. Built with Quarto and published via GitHub Pages (Option A, /docs on main).

title: Evidence-Based Engineering
text: 🚧 This website is actively being built. Some pages may be incomplete or move.

title: Evidence-Based Engineering
text:
Current Focus


Technology Assessment Framework
A reusable framework for evaluating wastewater treatment technologies using engineering performance metrics, statistical summaries, compliance assessment, reliability analysis, and conditional reliability analysis.
Learn more


Oxford Hills Case Study
A pilot case study applying the technology assessment framework to field monitoring data from the Oxford Hills wastewater treatment system, with emphasis on TIN reliability and demonstrated treatment capability.
View case study


Engineering Statistics
Practical statistical methods for civil and environmental engineering, including exploratory analysis, uncertainty, reliability, visualization, and reproducible reporting.
Explore methods


Regulatory Science
Evidence-based approaches to support transparent, consistent, and reproducible technical review within a modern regulator framework.
Read more

title: Evidence-Based Engineering
text:
Guiding Principle

Engineering decisions should be based on transparent evidence, reproducible analysis, and clear communication of uncertainty and reliability.

title: Evidence-Based Engineering
text:
Recent Development
The current priority is development of an Evidence-Based Technology Assessment Framework for wastewater treatment technologies. The framework is being piloted using the Oxford Hills dataset and is intended to support clear assessment of demonstrated technology capability based on submitted field monitoring data.
